rationale: Both candidates correctly tie the failure to #4834 (matching yenuo26's comment) and give the same plausible technical mechanism (wake_up guard on _level2_sleeping, stale test using level=2), but both confidently assert the fix is 'already on main, verified' and recommend closing — this contradicts the actual thread, where Flink-ddd is still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run to investigate, i.e. the issue was NOT established as resolved. That overreach caps correctness for both similarly. X is materially hurt by verbatim-duplicated draft_answer blocks (padding with no new information) and a thinner treatment of the fix's implications, while Y adds more grounded detail (the companion generate() RuntimeError guard, level-1 vs level-2 runtime guidance for users) and reads as a single coherent, complete narrative rather than a repeated report dump.
